title: cymbopogon excavatus
intro: Cymbopogon excavatus, also known as Java citronella, is a type of grass native to tropical Asia and Australia, belonging to the Poaceae family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains citronellal, citronellol, and geraniol, and is used to repel insects, reduce fever, and alleviate headaches. The health benefits of Cymbopogon excavatus include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. Cymbopogon excavatus is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
